highlights.text: I wrote last Tuesday January 14th: “My general lean is that a relief bounce leg began on Monday by testing megaphone support, and is underway.
I wrote this Tuesday at 4pm: “My general lean is always just to defer to the trend until it ends.
I was then looking for more upside today, writing yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend until evidence tells me otherwise.
As readers know (and as updated clearly at the end of every session), I was in the same long since the 6020 Failed Breakdown Monday evening, and this run will be tough to beat also.

Since I started this newsletter in 2021, I’ve devoted most of it to teaching readers exactly how rallies start, perpetuate, and end in ES (they are perpetuated by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown) As readers know, this rally started on Monday Jan 13th, when ES tested support of a 2 month long, “megaphone pattern” at 5810.
Then this week, its just been a matter of waiting for Failed Breakdowns, and looking higher each day.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed Breakdown we saw yesterday morning at 6115 which triggered this most recent leg up.

post_title: SPX Puts In A Rare Red Day. Will It Get Bought? Jan 27 Plan
